---
title: "Alberta Income Support: Caseload Forecast"
subtitle: "Project Overview"
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: simple
    slide-number: true
    preview-links: auto
---

## Project Overview

- **What**: End-to-end caseload forecasting pipeline for Alberta's Income Support program
- **Data**: Monthly Government of Alberta open data — April 2005 to September 2025 (~50,000 rows)
- **Output**: 24-month ARIMA forecast with 80% and 95% prediction intervals
- **Execution**: Fully reproducible — `Rscript flow.R` re-runs every stage from raw to report
- **Phase 2**: Cloud-agnostic on-prem core designed for migration to Azure ML and Snowflake

---

## Data Sources

- **Primary source**: [Alberta Open Data — Income Support Caseload](https://open.alberta.ca/opendata/income-support-aggregated-caseload-data)
- **Coverage**: April 2005 – September 2025 (246 months)
- **Dimensions**: Total caseload · Client type · Family composition · ALSS regions · Age groups · Gender
- **Format**: Monthly aggregates, publicly updated by the Government of Alberta
- **Ingestion**: Supports four equivalent sources — URL, CSV, SQLite, SQL Server (via Ferry pattern)

---

## Pipeline Architecture

```{mermaid}
flowchart LR
    subgraph "Data Ingestion"
        A["1-ferry.R<br/><i>Import</i>"]
    end
    subgraph "Transformation"
        B["2-ellis.R<br/><i>Clean & Shape</i>"]
    end
    subgraph "Advisory"
        C["eda-2.qmd<br/><i>Diagnostics</i>"]
    end
    subgraph "Modeling"
        D["3-mint-IS.R<br/><i>Prepare</i>"]
        E["4-train-IS.R<br/><i>Estimate</i>"]
        F["5-forecast-IS.R<br/><i>Predict</i>"]
    end
    subgraph "Delivery"
        G["report-1.qmd<br/><i>Report</i>"]
    end

    A --> B --> C
    B --> D --> E --> F --> G
    C -.->|informs| D

    style A fill:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    style B fill:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    style C fill:#f5a623,color:#fff
    style D fill:#7b68ee,color:#fff
    style E fill:#7b68ee,color:#fff
    style F fill:#7b68ee,color:#fff
    style G fill:#50c878,color:#fff
```

---

## Key EDA Findings

- **20-year trend**: Total caseload shows distinct structural breaks — 2008 financial crisis, 2015–16 oil price collapse, 2020 COVID-19 surge
- **Seasonality**: Clear annual cycle with peaks in winter months (Jan–Mar) and troughs in summer
- **Stationarity**: ADF and KPSS tests confirm the series is integrated of order 1 (I(1)) — one round of differencing required
- **Log transform**: Variance-stabilising log transform confirmed appropriate by EDA (reduces heteroscedasticity)
- **ARIMA order**: ACF/PACF analysis and STL decomposition informed selection of ARIMA(3,1,1)(1,0,0)[12]

---

## Forecast Results

![24-month ahead forecast — Alberta Income Support caseload](../../analysis/report-1/prints/g1_forecast_report.png){width=100%}

---

## Model Performance

- **Primary model**: ARIMA(3,1,1)(1,0,0)\[12\] on log-transformed total caseload
- **Baseline model**: Seasonal Naïve (last observed value propagated with seasonal adjustment)
- **Backtest**: 24-month held-out window — ARIMA outperforms Seasonal Naïve on RMSE, MAE, and MAPE
- **Prediction intervals**: 80% and 95% intervals generated for full 24-month horizon (Oct 2025 – Sep 2027)
- **Lineage**: Every model and forecast is linked to its exact data slice via `forge_hash` in `forge_manifest.yml`

---

## Next Steps

- **Phase 2 — Cloud migration**: Adapt on-prem pipeline to Azure ML (primary target) and Snowflake ML (secondary target)
- **Model registry**: Transition from local `.rds` files to cloud model registry with MLflow experiment tracking
- **Programme-level forecasts**: Re-graft onto production data with subgroup disaggregation (by client type, region)
- **Scheduled refresh**: Replace manual `Rscript flow.R` with cloud-scheduled monthly pipeline runs
- **Report delivery**: Migrate static HTML to cloud-hosted web app with identity-provider access control (Entra ID)
